steps.title: Identify the shift from stated impressions to the speaker's dismissal
steps.content: Lines 1–2 present impressions others have (“Let them think I love them more,” “Let them think I care”). Lines 3–4 dismiss their significance (“what is it to me / Who am self-complete”).

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer
steps.content:
D names this structure exactly: listing impressions others have, then explaining why they don't matter to the speaker.

The other choices either falsely claim the poem recounts scenarios, invent a tone of sadness or desire to connect, or overgeneralize the speaker's independence into a broad principle.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
